headline: All majors below 200-day lines, momentum still positive but price rolling over, whales moving coins to exchanges while stablecoin float shrinks.
paragraphs: The regime is decisively bearish on the daily structure. Bitcoin sits 47.6 percent below its 12-month high, Ethereum 60.3 percent underwater, and all seven major assets reside below their 200-day moving averages with no golden crosses in sight. Not one asset has reclaimed its longer-term trend line. The market cap dipped 0.34 percent over 24 hours to 2.317 trillion USD, and Bitcoin dominance stands at 56.6 percent, a baseline that suggests no capitulation rotation into alts has begun. This is a market defined by weakness across the board, not a selective washout.

The momentum picture adds texture to that decline. Bitcoin's RSI sits at 55.5 with MACD above its signal line, suggesting the downtrend has not yet exhausted itself but is not in free fall either. Ethereum's RSI at 62.0 is the highest among the majors, still above 50 and MACD-positive, meaning the selling pressure, while present, has not spawned oversold conditions. Solana is different: RSI at 51.7 with MACD below signal tells a softer picture, a coin treading water. Doge, however, shows strain at RSI 39.0, the lowest in the set, while still posting MACD-positive, a classic divergence between momentum collapse and trend structure. The selling today was real and broad, but the underlying momentum indicators have not yet capitulated. That matters for what happens next.

Derivatives positioning reveals a crowd that is still long but showing cracks. Bitcoin perp funding on OKX reads negative 0.0034 percent per 8 hours (negative 3.7 percent annualized), a bearish signal: shorts are being paid to hold as leverage buyers overhang the market. Open interest stands at 2.022 billion USD, substantial but not extreme. The long-to-short ratio is 1.26, meaning for every short contract, there are 1.26 longs, still a net long bias but not euphoric. Liquidations in the past 24 hours total 806 events across 4.987 million USD: more shorts got liquidated (2.687 million) than longs (2.299 million), typical behavior in a down market where overleveraged bulls get stopped. Ethereum has flipped: funding is positive at 0.0011 percent per 8 hours (1.2 percent annualized), shorts are paying longs to hold, and it has suffered harder liquidations (1.6 billion events, 13.7 million USD total), more shorts than longs again. The positioning is crowded on the long side still, but the funding structure is signaling caution from leverage traders.

Whale flows tell a blunt story: coins moving toward exchanges, not away. Over the past 72 hours, whales moved 499.1 million USD of Bitcoin onto exchanges while only 64.7 million came off, a net inflow of 434.5 million. The two largest single moves were 171.4 million to OKEx and 134.4 million to Kraken, the places where coins are sold. The only offsetting move was 64.7 million removed from Coinbase. In the same window, 118.5 million USD of stablecoin (USDT) moved onto OKEx, dry powder arriving ahead of a potential buy opportunity. Yet the weekly trend shows accumulation turning to distribution: the past four weeks read positive 1.89 billion, positive 996 million, negative 147.9 million, then negative 333.7 million. The direction has flipped. This is not yet a full capitulation flow (those read much larger and faster), but it is the beginning of patience running out on the long side.

Stablecoin float stands at 307.4 billion USD, down 1.75 percent over 30 days, a slow erosion of dry powder as traders either buy or withdraw capital. Fear and Greed reads 31, deep in fear territory and the lowest point of its 30-day range (11 to 33), meaning the crowd's anxiety is near peak. This is where sentiment gets interesting: the gauge screams fear, the technical structure confirms downtrend, yet funding on Bitcoin is paid shorts and the liquidations are tilted toward longs. Whales are selling into this, moving coins to venues in the past 72 hours, but the weekly trend has only just begun to roll. The contradiction is not whether the market is weak (it clearly is) but whether this selling is nearly finished or just starting.

Watch for a flip in Bitcoin funding back toward positive, which would signal shorts are in control and the market has priced in deeper pain. Watch also for the pace of whale inflows to accelerate or stabilize, the difference between testing support and breaking it. The 200-day line on Bitcoin sits around 69,000 USD, roughly 6 percent above current price; a reclaim would change the daily regime in an instant. Until one of those conditions shifts, the tape reads as weakness continuing against a backdrop of technicals that have not yet given full surrender signals.
